title: analytics — entitlements
description: Nine entitlements gating analytics capture + delivery + advanced features. Plan-tier scaled: Small (baseline), Medium (advanced providers + batch config), Enterprise (identity merge + sampling config + extended retention + session recording).

entitlements.description: Master gate. Off = analytics module refuses capture entirely (no events, no deliveries, no identity resolution). Free tenants have client-side @stackra/analytics still; server-side is gated behind this.
Number of active AnalyticsProviderConfig rows allowed per tenant. Consumed on create; released on soft-delete.

Monthly event dispatch pool. One AnalyticsEvent captured = one draw regardless of eligible_provider_count. Reset at UTC month boundary. Overage refuses NEW captures with NOTIFICATIONS_ANALYTICS_MONTHLY_QUOTA_EXCEEDED.
Enables Mixpanel + Segment + June + Custom Webhook providers. Small tenants only get PostHog + Amplitude + Google Analytics 4 (the baseline three).
Tenant may override batch_config.size + flush_interval_ms + max_bytes per provider. Without this entitlement, batch_config is frozen to the provider's default from data/batch-defaults.json.

Enables cross-device identity merge via POST /identities/{id}/merge + the alias() verb. Without this entitlement, identify() still writes an AnalyticsIdentity row but historical events are NOT rewritten to the identified user id — the two remain disjoint subject histories.
Enables custom sampling_rate + sampling_rules per provider. Non-Enterprise tenants can only sample via PostHog (which is free to sample locally) and Custom Webhook (opt-in escape hatch).
Extends AnalyticsEvent retention from 2 years to 5 years. Deliveries retention remains 90 days regardless. Enterprise + regulatory-heavy tenants.

Enables PostHog session recording capture. Independently gated by the `functional` consent category on the subject — an Enterprise tenant may have the entitlement but the recording still refuses if the subject hasn't granted functional consent.
